title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Desperate to fight Ebola outbreak, Congo weighs using longshot vaccine options
items.description: Existing vaccines may offer some protection against the rare strain now circulating—but the evidence is scant

items.title: Court blocks NSF’s transfer of climate lab’s supercomputing facility
items.description: Proposed switch in management caused irreparable harm to NCAR, judge finds

items.title: Exclusive: HHS is now weighing in on science in NIH grants
items.description: Staffers say comments coming after NIH’s own approvals are overriding peer review

items.title: ‘That could be a game changer’: antiviral pill gets first test for Ebola prevention
items.description: Researchers are preparing to launch an unprecedented trial to protect people exposed to Ebola in the DRC and Uganda

items.title: White House seeks to tighten political oversight of grantmaking
items.description: Sweeping proposed rule, now open for comments, would also restrict foreign collaborations and remove federal funding for open-access fees

items.title: Brazil’s ‘rolling stone reefs’ teem with hidden diversity, DNA reveals
items.description: Vast offshore habitats face threats including mining, trawling, and drilling for oil

items.title: ‘Mind-blowing’: Iron-rich immune cells help homing pigeons navigate
items.description: New study identifies a surprising mechanism for sensing Earth’s magnetic field

items.title: New drug ‘functionally cures’ many hepatitis B virus infections
items.description: GSK’s “bepi” performs better than expected in efficacy trials, but its global impact is likely limited

items.title: Some spinosaurs cried salty tears to thrive in brackish waters
items.description: Fossil evidence suggests some predatory dinosaurs could expel salt from their bloodstream like modern birds and crocodiles do

items.title: Surveys capture the pulsing of mantle plumes—a potential cause of mass extinctions
items.description: Seismic imaging and drilling cruises bolster a controversial explanation for ancient surges in warming
